mission.title: Monthly TLS Certificate Expiry Check
mission.description: Recurring orbit mission that scans for TLS certificates approaching expiration. Checks cert-manager Certificate resources, Kubernetes TLS secrets, and ingress certificates to prevent unexpected outages from expired certs.

steps.title: Check cert-manager Certificate resources
steps.description: List all cert-manager Certificate resources and their renewal status. Certificates with Ready=False or approaching notAfter dates need attention.
commands: kubectl get certificates -A -o wide 2>/dev/null || echo 'cert-manager CRDs not installed'

kubectl get certificates -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.namespace}/{.metadata.name} notAfter={.status.notAfter} ready={range .status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")]}{.status}{end}{"\n"}{end}' 2>/dev/null

validation.command: kubectl get certificates -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{range .status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")]}{.status}{"\n"}{end}{end}' 2>/dev/null | grep -c False || echo 0

steps.title: Scan TLS secrets for expiring certificates
steps.description: Extract and check expiration dates from all TLS-type secrets in the cluster. Flag any expiring within 30 days.
commands: for secret in $(kubectl get secrets -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[?(@.type=="kubernetes.io/tls")]}{.metadata.namespace}/{.metadata.name}{"\n"}{end}'); do ns=$(echo $secret | cut -d/ -f1); name=$(echo $secret | cut -d/ -f2); expiry=$(kubectl get secret $name -n $ns -o jsonpath='{.data.tls\.crt}' | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -enddate 2>/dev/null | cut -d= -f2); echo "$secret expires: $expiry"; done

steps.title: Check ingress TLS configuration
steps.description: Verify that all ingresses with TLS blocks reference valid, non-expired secrets.

steps.title: Verify cert-manager issuer health
steps.description: Check that ClusterIssuers and Issuers are in a Ready state so new certificates can be issued when renewals are needed.
commands: kubectl get clusterissuers -o wide 2>/dev/null || echo 'No ClusterIssuers found'
kubectl get issuers -A -o wide 2>/dev/null || echo 'No Issuers found'

validation.command: kubectl get clusterissuers -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{range .status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")]}{.status}{"\n"}{end}{end}' 2>/dev/null | grep -c False || echo 0

steps.title: Review certificate events for renewal failures
steps.description: Check for recent cert-manager events that indicate failed renewals or issuance problems.

resolution.summary: TLS certificate expiry check complete. All certificates, secrets, and issuers have been reviewed for upcoming expirations and health status.

prerequisites.description: Requires kubectl with cluster access. openssl is needed to decode certificate expiry dates. cert-manager CRDs are optional but recommended.
